Multi-language Quarto: R + Python

Author

Z. Wang

Markdown + Inline Math

You can type markdown freely, and LaTex style inline math like \(\mu\), \(\sigma^2\), or display math like \(\mu\) \(a + b \neq b + a\)

\[ log(\frac{p}{1-p})=\beta_0 + \beta_1x_1 + \beta_2 x_2 \]

Bold, italic, hyperlink, list, table, figure, citation, etc. just like in normal Markdown.

  • bullet 1

  • bullet 2

Col1 Col2 Col3
1 1
2 2
3 3

Figure

R chunk

Code 
# R code example

library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data = cars, aes(x = speed, y = dist)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",linetype = "dashed", color = "firebrick") +
  labs(
    title = "Stopping Distance vs. Speed",
    x = "Speed (mph)",
    y = "Stopping Distance (ft)"
  )
Code 
# call python
library(reticulate)

py_config()
python:         /Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate/bin/python
libpython:      /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/config-3.11-darwin/libpython3.11.dylib
pythonhome:     /Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate:/Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate
version:        3.11.3 (v3.11.3:f3909b8bc8, Apr  4 2023, 20:12:10) [Clang 13.0.0 (clang-1300.0.29.30)]
numpy:          /Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate/lib/python3.11/site-packages/numpy
numpy_version:  2.3.3
Code 
py_install("pandas") # install python package in r
Using virtual environment '/Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate' ...
Code 
py_install("matplotlib")
Using virtual environment '/Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate' ...

Python chunk:

Code 
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# small df
df = pd.DataFrame({"x": np.arange(5), "y": [3,1,4,1,5]})


# Create a basic line plot
plt.plot(df.x, df.y, marker="o")      

plt.xlabel("x")
plt.ylabel("y")
plt.title("Line Plot Example")

# Show the plot window
plt.show()

Transfer Data between R and Python

Write and Read

Code 
# write.csv(cars, "cars.csv", row.names = FALSE)
Code 
import pandas as pd
data = pd.read_csv("cars.csv")
data.head()
speed dist
0 4 2
1 4 10
2 7 4
3 7 22
4 8 16

Or direct send your object to Python

Code 
# Send to Python

py$cars <- cars         # assign to Python

check function r_to_python and python_to_r
